content_text: Thank you to the folks of Union County for welcoming Tech Goes Home into your community!Let’s begin Round 2!!! Today we started round 2 of our ‘Tech Goes Home Tennessee’ training for our senior citizens. We had 11 participants. At the end of the training, if they participate in all 15 hours, they get to take home their Chromebooks after our celebration/graduation. This program was made possible because of a partnership with our Mayor Jason Bailey’s office and ETDD (East TN Development District). The next class is already full but if you’re a senior and you want to join our July 6-10, call Wendy or Joan at 865-992-3292

Joe is a great example of age just being a number! At 92 he's leading our Anderson County TGH classes and helping local residents feel more and more comfortable with technology!Today was Google Chromebook graduation with Joe Harper (certified instructor) at the helm! Congratulations to everyone. We are so proud of class 2!

ChaTech is excited to partner with The Enterprise Center to help connect our community with emerging technology through the Tech A Look At workshop series.

This month's session focuses on Claude, one of today's leading AI tools. Participants will gain a practical understanding of what Claude is, how it works, and how organizations and individuals are using AI in real-world settings.

If you're looking for an accessible introduction to AI and its practical applications, this workshop is a great opportunity to learn and engage with others in the community.

👉 Learn more/register: https://buff.ly/2rTasI5 

#AI #Claude #Innovation #TechCommunity #Chattanooga

As part of our Viable Pathways to Prosperity initiative, we’re #hiring two new roles to help expand career-connected learning, apprenticeship pathways, and economic opportunity for young people across Hamilton County:

These positions will support a new national skilled trades initiative focused on connecting students to high-demand, thriving-wage careers through strong partnerships across education, workforce, labor, and industry.

If you’re passionate about expanding educational opportunity, workforce development, and helping young people build meaningful futures, visit the link below to learn more and apply by June 9: https://lnkd.in/eFXHayxt

#PathwaysToProsperity #OperationsManager

Looking forward to this series!Staying up to date with the latest technologies can get overwhelming. That’s why we are introducing a new workshop to demystify it all. Tech A Look offers bite-sized, one hour workshops on emerging technology topics led by guest experts. Whether you’re curious about AI, cybersecurity, automation, quantum computing, or the future of logistics, these sessions provide an accessible way to learn about the technologies transforming our world. Our first of the series will focus on the basics of Claude and real world applications. Join us and our guest speaker, Russell McBride, at 10am on June 9th at The Enterprise Center. 🎟️ For more details + free registration —> https://bit.ly/techalook-claude 🗓️ June 9th, 10am-11am 📍The Enterprise Center,  1010 Georgia Ave, Ste 300 Chattanooga, TN 37402

Confidence can be transformative. Our signature professional attire and styling services, paired with interview preparation and confidence coaching, equip women to present themselves with confidence and authenticity as they step into new opportunities. 

Dress for Success Chattanooga delivers more than a moment of support – we provide a comprehensive community of resources and services. We recognize that economic mobility is not achieved through a single intervention, but through sustained, integrated support over time. 

We DREAM for Success, DRESS for Success, DEVELOP for Success, DRIVE for Success, and DONATE for Success. 

Join Us in Igniting Careers and Transforming: donate at www.dfschattanooga.org

TONIGHT! We’re taking YPC After Hours to Whiskey Thief for an evening of connection in an elevated space overlooking the Scenic City. Whether you’re looking to expand your network or just unwind with other young professionals, this is the place to be.

Come ready to grab a drink, meet someone new, and enjoy a laid-back atmosphere where connections happen naturally.

Register here: https://ow.ly/Zy5550YW81G

My name is Zac, I’m 29 years old and the proud father of a beautiful baby girl. After high school, I had the opportunity to play collegiate golf, but I only remained for one semester, as choices I made at the time led me away from the sport and my education. For years, those choices left me struggling and significantly impacted my ability to stay focused and committed, and began to shape nearly every part of my life.  Today, I’m incredibly grateful to have a renewed sense of purpose and clarity. Through the support of my family and the Tennessee Reconnect program, I’ve been given the chance to start again with intention.  Without a degree, I found myself limited in the opportunities available. I worked hard with little growth, stability, or fulfillment. Those experiences showed me how education can open doors—professionally and personally. I have a deep gratitude for TN Reconnect which has given my family and me something incredibly meaningful: hope for a more stable and fulfilling future. I want my daughter to look at me and see someone who didn’t give up, even after making mistakes and facing difficult seasons. Because of TN Reconnect, I believe in my education again, in myself, and in what’s possible for my family. To anyone who is just starting school, returning after time away, or even considering leaving—please know that the choices you make today truly matter, and you owe it to yourself to try your best. Learn more about FREE tuition with the TN Reconnect Grant and the support ChattState provides adult students at chattanoogastate.edu/tnreconnect

Project Ready is gearing up for another AMAZING year, and we are excited to welcome back our returning student leaders! Both returners and those who are interested in joining Project Ready, please complete the application as soon as possible.  Spots are limited!  Please submit your application by Monday, June 15th, to be considered for our class of 2026-2027 cohort. We look forward to another year of leadership, growth, college and career opportunities, service, and unforgettable experiences. Thank you for being part of the Project Ready family. We can’t wait to see what you achieve next! Application Link:  https://docs.google.com/…/1FAIpQLSdIZb1feIp82m9YNK…/viewform

Build Your Business Quick Tip: Host in-house events to build community among your customers.  In-house events do more than bring people through your doors. They turn customers into a community. From workshops and networking nights to VIP previews and special experiences, hosting events creates meaningful connections with your brand and between your customers. The result? Stronger relationships, increased loyalty, and a business people remember long after the sale.  Featured: Plant Bar Chattanooga

What an incredible day of celebration at the #MMCTulsa graduation 🎓✨ Seeing our students cross the stage, hearing their stories, and watching their hard work pay off reminds us why we do what we do. This class showed determination, resilience, and heart! every step of the way. We couldn’t be more proud of each and every graduate. To our graduates: congratulations on believing in yourselves and pushing forward. Your journey is just getting started, and we can’t wait to see the impact you make in your careers.💙🙌 Ready to start YOUR future? Contact us today to learn how #MMCTulsa can help you take the first step toward a rewarding career. 📍3801 S Sheridan Rd, Tulsa, OK 74145 📞(918) 221-3435 #MMCTulsa #GraduationDay #CareerTraining #TradeSchool #Tulsa #StartYourFuture

The Learning Agility Accelerator is back! We’re excited to launch another cohort of the Learning Agility Accelerator! LAA is a FREE professional development program designed for entry- to mid-level professionals in the Chattanooga area looking to strengthen their digital and workplace technology skills. Over this 15-hour, 3-week experience, participants will learn practical skills for using AI and other digital tools in the workplace. Participants who successfully complete the program will receive a FREE computer. 📍 Lupton Building – 1010 Georgia Ave, Floor 3, Chattanooga, TN 📅 Tuesdays & Thursdays | 5:30 PM – 8:30 PM 🗓️ July 7 – July 21, 2026 Apply here: https://forms.gle/UmrDsSK4T6nWi5vL7 Spots are limited — we’re excited to continue helping Chattanooga professionals build confidence with technology and prepare for the evolving digital workplace.

We’re excited to feature Annea Robinson at Scenic City Summit 2026.

🎤 Rebooting the City: How to Modernize Culture and Prepare for AI

In this session, Annea will share the real-world story behind transforming a legacy government technology department through rapid hiring, modernization, and enterprise AI adoption.

From building a high-performing technology team inside the public sector to establishing governance and responsible AI practices in partnership with Google, this talk offers practical leadership insights for organizations navigating change and innovation at scale.

🎟️ Secure your ticket: https://buff.ly/29psw0Q 

#SCS2026 #ScenicCitySummit #AI #GovTech #Leadership #Innovation

We’re excited to officially launch the Southeast Tennessee Development District’s monthly newsletter! 🎉

This newsletter will serve as a new way for us to keep our communities, partners, stakeholders, and residents informed about the impactful work happening across Southeast Tennessee.

Subscribers can expect updates on:
✅ Economic and community development initiatives
✅ Regional projects and partnerships
✅ Aging & disability services programs provided through SETAAD
✅ Updates on programs and services through the Southeast Tennessee Workforce Development Board
✅ Grant opportunities and funding news
✅ Success stories, community spotlights, and exciting developments from across our region

Our goal is to provide a consistent, transparent, and engaging look at the work being done at SETD to strengthen Southeast Tennessee and support the communities we serve.

📬 The newsletter will be delivered on the second Wednesday of each month, so be on the lookout in your inboxes! 

We’d love for you to follow along and stay connected. Sign up below to join our mailing list and receive future updates directly in your inbox!

https://lnkd.in/d7SFXDPB

Chattanooga State’s Heavy Equipment Operator Course is preparing individuals with the skills employers are actively seeking—and the results speak for themselves. Several Chattanooga-area construction companies are already offering job possibilities to students upon successful completion of the program.

 Program & Events Coordinator, Economic & Workforce Development
 Chattanooga State Community College
Email: kristi.mead@chattanoogastate.edu Phone: (423)697-2514

When people think about healthcare careers, nursing often comes to mind first—but there are many ways to make a difference in healthcare. From fast-paced hospitals to small private offices, healthcare careers offer a wide variety of work environments and opportunities. This summer, explore healthcare career paths at ChattState info sessions for: 🩺 EMS 💙 Nursing (RN) 💊 Pharmacy Tech 🦷 Dental Hygiene 🐾 Veterinary Tech ☢️ Nuclear Medicine Learn about program requirements, career opportunities, and how you can build a future helping others.  Get more info on all Nursing and Allied Health programs at https://ow.ly/JclG50Z2bJ2

You’re invited to join us for a fascinating case study exploring the extraordinary story behind Empty Mansions and the lasting estate and philanthropic planning lessons it offers today. 

Through the work of Chattanooga native and Pulitzer Prize–winning journalist, Bill Dedman, and attorney Jennifer Exum, participants will engage in a thought-provoking discussion designed for financial advisors and estate planning professionals—plus earn 1.5 CLE credits! 

RSVP today by emailing LCross@cfgc.org

#PartnerForGood #MakeMorePossible

Do you believe every child deserves to learn how to read—and that well-supported tutors can help make that possible?

Chattanooga 2.0 is #hiring a Program Manager for an AmeriCorps-funded early literacy tutoring program that places trained tutors in some of Hamilton County’s highest-need elementary schools.

The AmeriCorps program manager will support AmeriCorps volunteers, coordinate across schools, track implementation closely, and help ensure our students receive consistent, high-quality reading support every day.

For more information and how to apply, visit: https://lnkd.in/ehCsQJ4Y

#AmeriCorps #VolunteerTennessee

✨ Monday Motivation: Kendra’s Story ✨ Are you thinking about online training, but worried you might miss out on the support of an in-person classroom? Kendra Parker is here to tell you that at MMC, you are not alone! Kendra has already completed her both Medical Billing and Coding and Health Information Technology programs online and had this to say about the support she received: “The support you have online is just like being in person. Whenever I felt overwhelmed with work, life, and school, I always had somebody right there for me...” Don’t let a busy schedule stand in the way of your dreams. You can balance your daily responsibilities while gaining an education through our online platform. If you prefer a face-to-face experience, we also offer hands-on training at our campus locations. ▶️ Listen to the rest of Kendra’s story here: https://www.youtube.com/watch…  Take Kendra’s advice and just “DO IT!” Your MMC family is ready to help you start. 👉 miller-motte.edu 📲 423-510-2771 #MondayMotivation #GradStory #HealthcareTraining #OnlineLearning #MillerMotteCollege #MMCOnline

From the classroom to the trails, UTC students are helping care for Chattanooga’s outdoor spaces. 🌲🥾 Students in UTC’s new Trail Building and Management course are gaining hands-on experience by clearing trails, removing hazards and helping restore access to local recreation areas throughout the community. The course combines outdoor recreation, conservation and real-world workforce training while helping meet a growing need for trail management and stewardship in Chattanooga. Read more: https://blog.utc.edu/…/the-chattanooga-trails-utc-program-…/

We’re excited to feature Chris Gonzalez at Scenic City Summit 2026.

🎤 Zero To Agent: A Beginner’s Guide to AWS AgentCore and Strands SDK

This session is designed for developers interested in AI agents but unsure where to begin. Chris will demonstrate how to build and deploy a working AI agent using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore and the Strands SDK with minimal setup and infrastructure overhead.

Attendees will gain practical insight into core agent concepts, custom tooling, and deploying scalable AI-powered workflows in AWS.

🎟️ Secure your ticket: https://buff.ly/29psw0Q 

#SCS2026 #ScenicCitySummit #AI #AWS #Cloud #SoftwareDevelopment

We were pleased to attend Hamilton County Government Mayor Weston Wamp's State of the County address, hosted earlier this week at The University of Tennessee at Chattanooga.

From career readiness to outdoor recreation, Mayor Wamp addressed several initiatives that enhance community success and quality of life in Hamilton County and the surrounding region.

A panel discussion with Mayor Wamp, State Senator Bo Watson, TNECD Commissioner Stuart McWhorter, TDOT Commissioner Will Reid, and TDEC Commissioner David Salyers underscored the importance of county/state collaboration and highlighted several topics that impact the tri-state, including:

📌Prioritization and investment in infrastructure projects that quality of life and the economy.

📌Career and technical education that supports workforce development for industry in the region.

The "economic ecosystem" of outdoor recreation that blends history, natural resources, and wildlife as Hamilton County introduces McDonald Farm, the county's fifth state park.

Thank you for the thoughtful conversation! We look forward to continuing to support communities across Hamilton County and the tri-state as they create places people are proud to call home for generations to come.

We’re excited to feature Grant Fritchey at Scenic City Summit 2026.

🎤 The DBA Who Watched Planes: A Real World AI + PostgreSQL Project

This session explores a real-world AI and data engineering project built around publicly available airplane transponder data. Grant will walk through how data is captured, transmitted to Azure, stored in PostgreSQL, and integrated with Large Language Models to provide live updates.

Blending practical architecture with a fun use case, this session highlights how AI, cloud infrastructure, and databases can come together in creative and meaningful ways.

🎟️ Secure your ticket: https://buff.ly/29psw0Q 

#SCS2026 #ScenicCitySummit #AI #PostgreSQL #Cloud #DataEngineering
